title: melaleuca zonalis
intro: Melaleuca zonalis, also known as the coastal tea tree, is a species of plant in the myrtle family, native to Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to the presence of compounds such as cineole and limonene. The health benefits of Melaleuca zonalis include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Melaleuca zonalis include tea, essential oil, and tincture, which can be used to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
